entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: A region characterized by highly variable low seasonal rainfall where agricultural production needs to be stabilized

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of generating crops in the semi-arid tropics, which the ICRISAT aims to increase and stabilize

entities.name: SEASONAL RAINFALL
entities.description: The variable precipitation patterns that characterize the semi-arid tropics

entities.name: CLIMATIC CLASSIFICATION
entities.description: Generalized approaches used to distinguish and categorize different climates

entities.name: TROLL CLASSIFICATION
entities.description: A specific climatic classification approach suggested by Troll to distinguish climates

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: Precipitation data used as a basis for mapping the dry semi-arid tropics

entities.name: EVAPORATION
entities.description: Water loss data used as a basis for mapping the dry semi-arid tropics

entities.name: AGROCLIMATIC CLASSIFICATION
entities.description: A method of classifying the semi-arid tropics to address agricultural risks, droughts, and soil moisture

entities.name: CROPS
entities.description: Agricultural plants that are subject to risks identified through agroclimatic classification

entities.name: CROPPING PATTERNS
entities.description: The spatial and temporal arrangement of crops, which are subject to risks identified through agroclimatic classification

entities.name: DROUGHTS
entities.description: Intra- and inter-seasonal periods of water deficiency that agroclimatic classification aims to address

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE
entities.description: The amount of water in the seeding zone, critical for the success of dry seeding

entities.name: DRY SEEDING
entities.description: The agricultural practice of sowing seeds into soil that lacks sufficient moisture
